item.title: Version 0.0.32
item.description:
• **Beta channel.** Settings → Updates now has a "Receive beta updates" toggle. Off by default — new releases land on the beta lane first and are promoted to stable only after a maintainer has smoke-tested them. Flip the toggle on to get pre-release builds early; switch it back off any time and you'll stick to stable from the next update onward.
  • **Refreshed UI.** Slack-style redesign across all surfaces — bigger readable fonts, the SF Pro system font throughout, cleaner dropdowns, and a new Recent tab.
  • **Releases are no longer instantly stable.** Until now every tagged release was visible to every user the moment CI finished. Going forward each release goes to the beta channel first; stable users only see it after a maintainer promotes it. This costs a few hours of lag in exchange for a working rollback path when something breaks. *(v0.0.32 itself is being promoted to stable on day one so existing users get the channel-aware code.)*
  • **"Advanced" tab is now "Models".** Settings tab renamed for clarity — that tab is where you pick which model handles each role.
  • **No more hang on VPN startup.** A network-path edge case (VPN split-tunnel, restricted loopback under some MDM profiles, firewall intercepting localhost) used to make the local bridge stall for ~22 seconds at launch, which macOS sometimes killed as "CastWord Quit Unexpectedly". Now the bridge moves on within a millisecond.
  • E2E test coverage expanded from 95 → 180 assertions, split into P0 (every push) and P1 (nightly) tiers.
  • Historical testing plan from May 5 cherry-picked into `docs/testing/` for posterity.

item.title: Version 0.0.31
item.description:
• **Dictation works again out of the box.** v0.0.30 shipped with a model default that returned empty transcripts for new installs. Default is back to `whisper-1` for transcription and `gpt-4.1-nano` for cleanup, and if your API key is missing the overlay now tells you so instead of silently producing nothing.
  • **Hotkey doesn't fire on sleep / lock combos.** Hold-to-talk hotkeys now debounce key-down by 150ms, so the three-key chords macOS uses for sleep / lock screen don't accidentally trigger a recording.
  • **ESC actually cancels.** ESC during a recording now routes through the same cancel path as the 60-second watchdog, so the in-progress transcription is dropped instead of leaking through to paste.
  • **Simpler Settings.** Each provider/role now maps to one model — the per-row model dropdown is gone. Tone presets stay (they shape the LLM cleanup, not the model choice).
  • **Action mode overlay shows what was heard.** While Action mode is recording, the overlay streams a live "Heard: …" partial transcript so you can see whether your command landed before it executes.
  • Split text-mode and realtime logs to separate streams, plus optional Axiom remote shipping for diagnostics, plus app-match guards on plugin activation.

item.title: Version 0.0.30
item.description:
• **Smarter command planning.** Action mode now plans multi-step requests with an LLM ("set a reminder for 5pm to call mom and then open Notes") instead of guessing one tool at a time, so chained commands run in the right order.
  • **Reminder time parsing.** Phrases like "in 20 minutes", "at 5pm", or "tomorrow at 9" now turn into the correct reminder time without you having to spell it out.
  • **Safe Mode.** A toggle in Settings → Action mode that blocks disruptive voice actions (sending messages, deleting files, etc.) until you switch it off — useful while you're getting comfortable with Action mode.
  • **Clearer overlay during Action mode.** The overlay now shows what was heard, what's running, and a visible cancel target — so you always know how to stop a run mid-flight.
  • **Cancel works mid-transcription.** The overlay's cancellable state is restored across every recording stage, with a unified layout no matter what stage Action mode is in.
  • **Reminders no longer hang the app.** Setting up a reminder now validates Automation permission first, runs off the main thread, and re-arms the hotkey tap if the underlying Reminders call gets stuck.
  • **Hold-to-talk respects your setting again.** Mouse + Action mode hotkeys honour `hotkey_style = hold` instead of falling back to toggle behaviour.
  • **Better dictation accuracy.** Realtime transcription now receives your language hint and preserves a standalone "I" as a capital letter.

item.title: Version 0.0.29
item.description:
• **Faster start of recording.** Holding a hotkey now starts the mic immediately; the pre-flight checks (audio settings, device snapshot) run in the background instead of blocking the first words you say.
  • Local `./build.sh` now reverts the auto-bumped patch number if the compile / codesign / install step fails, so a transient keychain hiccup no longer skips version numbers in the source plist.

item.title: Version 0.0.28
item.description: See the full release notes on GitHub.

item.title: Version 0.0.27
item.description: See the full release notes on GitHub.

item.title: Version 0.0.24
item.description:
• **Groq for cleanup.** Settings → Advanced → LLM Cleanup now has an OpenAI / Groq toggle, matching the existing Text Mode picker. Pick Groq, paste a `gsk-…` key, and cleanup runs on `llama-3.1-8b-instant` (fastest) or `llama-3.3-70b-versatile` (highest quality). Default stays OpenAI — opt in to switch.
  • **Latency stats.** A new card in Settings → Advanced shows mean ASR / LLM polish / paste / post-speech end-to-end across your last 10 dictations, so you can see where time is going and decide if a different model would help.
  • **Personal Dictionary in Settings.** Silent correction learning is now on by default; the captured corrections show up in Settings → Customization where you can review or remove them.
  • Every dictation also writes a `[Latency]` line to `/tmp/castword/debug.log` for diagnostics.

item.title: Version 0.0.23
item.description:
• **See your current version next to Check for Updates.** Settings now shows "Current version: 0.0.23 (build 23)" right beside the *Check for Updates…* button, so you can tell at a glance whether what Sparkle is offering is actually newer than what you're running.
  • Auto-update publish is now fully automated end-to-end — the appcast that drives Sparkle gets signed and pushed by the release pipeline directly, without a manual post-step.

item.title: Version 0.0.22
item.description:
• **Pick the model for each mode.** Text mode and Action mode now have their own model dropdowns in Settings (OpenAI + Groq, with pricing shown so you can see the trade-off).
  • **Context-aware transcription.** The dictation pass takes hints from the app you're in and your recent topics, so names and jargon you've used before come back correctly more often.
  • **Personalized formatting.** Per-app presets and a tone setting shape how your transcripts are punctuated and structured (e.g. terse code-comment style in your editor, full sentences in Slack).
  • **Audio-quality diagnostics.** A new in-app meter shows mic level + a one-line preflight check before recording, so silent-mic and wrong-device cases are caught up front.
  • **Correction learning.** When you edit a transcript, CastWord remembers the change and applies it next time — scoped per app with a loop guard so it can't drift into bad rules.
  • **Uncertainty signals.** Low-confidence words from the recogniser are flagged and the rare full-reject case is handled gracefully instead of silently producing garbage.
  • **Window-title sanitization.** Sensitive bits of window titles (filenames, URLs, etc.) are now redacted before being used as context, with a visible audit panel.
  • **Selection-context badge.** When CastWord uses your selected text for a command, a small badge shows what's being read.
  • **No more launch crash.** Resolved a SIGABRT (Swift exclusive-access violation) some users hit shortly after launch.
  • **Hotkey doesn't fire while you're rebinding it.** Capturing a new hotkey in Settings no longer triggers the existing one.
  • **API key from environment.** If `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set in your environment, CastWord uses it on first launch instead of starting blank.

item.title: Version 0.0.21
item.description: • **Auto-update loop.** Sparkle's "An update is available" prompt no longer comes back after you install the latest version. (The build number stamped inside the app now matches the build number the appcast advertises, so Sparkle correctly recognises the update as installed.)

item.title: Version 0.0.20
item.description:
• **ESC cancels a recording.** Tap ESC while a hotkey is held and the in-progress recording is dropped — no transcription, no paste, nothing happens.
  • **Faster dictation.** Switched the transcription model to gpt-4o-mini-transcribe with streaming, so text appears noticeably sooner after you stop talking.
  • **Quieter Action Mode.** Tightened the Realtime prompt and stopped a class of duplicate tool-call firings that could happen right after a fast-path command (e.g. "open Safari" followed by a stray model-driven retry).
  • **Cleaner text mode.** The Stop button no longer appears during dictation, and the migration popup that used to interrupt some users on launch is suppressed.
  • **Status overlay on multi-monitor setups.** The overlay now shows on whichever screen your mouse cursor is on, instead of always pinning to the primary display.
  • **Corrections / snippets actually apply.** The text fixes you set in Settings (e.g. `teh = the`) now reach the cleanup pass instead of being silently dropped, and they're also fed to the LLM as soft hints.
